title: Unfortunately Yours
author: Tessa Bailey
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Unfortunately Yours" book by Tessa Bailey?
Options:
A. Back in New York she was unwanted. In St. Helena she was treated like royalty, which was absurd. Natalie took off her pajama shirt and got into the hot shower. And if the thought of her brother having sex was an unwanted image, it was nothing compared to the memory of August Cates yesterday, naked in all his muscular attractiveness. Natalie didn't have unlimited money like some wealthy people in this city. If only she did. Natalie really had nothing to complain about. She was living in a nice guest house on a vineyard. But she had been living off her savings for over a month now, and she could barely start a lemonade stand, let alone launch a company, with the little money she had left.
B. In New York Natalie was a nobody, but in St. Helena she was treated like a queen, hilariously. She slipped off her pajama top and stepped into the hot shower. If picturing her brother doing it was an unwanted image, it was nothing like the memory of hunky August Cates in his birthday suit yesterday. Natalie didn't have a bottomless bank account like certain rich folks in this city, though she wished she did. Truthfully Natalie had zero to complain about, staying in a lovely guest house on a winery property. But after draining her savings for over a month, she could hardly launch a business, never mind a lemonade stand, with the puny amount she had left.
C. Back in New York? Persona non grata. In St. Helena? Royalty. Snort. Natalie shed her sleepshirt and stepped beneath the hot shower spray. And if she thought her brother doing the deed constituted an unwanted image, it had nothing on the memory of August Cates yesterday afternoon in all his beefcake glory. I dont have a bottomless bank account like some people in this town. If only. Natalie didnt have anything to complain about. She was living in a beautiful guest house on the grounds of a vineyard, for gods sake. But shed been living off her savings for more than a month now and she could barely open a lemonade stand, let alone launch a firm, with the amount left over.
D. Natalie had gone from being unwanted in New York to being treated like royalty in St. Helena, which she found ridiculous. She took off her sleepshirt and got into the hot shower. The unwanted image of her brother having sex was nothing compared to the memory of the beefy, naked August Cates she had seen yesterday. Natalie didn't have the endless money supply that some wealthy people in this city had. She wished she did. Natalie really had nothing to complain about, living in a beautiful guest house on a vineyard. But after living off her savings for over a month, she barely had enough left to open a lemonade stand, let alone start a company. 
Answer:

Natalie didn't have unlimited money like some wealthy people in this city. If only she did. Natalie really had nothing to complain about. She was living in a nice guest house on a vineyard. But she had been living off her savings for over a month now, and she could barely start a lemonade stand, let alone launch a company, with the little money she had left.
